disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Intense scalp itching, worse at night, causing two nights of lost sleep in a young child
trigger_question_examples: What's been happening with her head?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Her head is so itchy she can't sleep — it's much worse at night and she's been scratching for two nights straight.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sensation of tickling/crawling movement in the hair, and the caregiver reports seeing tiny things move when the hair is parted
trigger_question_examples: Does it feel like anything is moving in her hair?
When you part her hair in good light, do you see anything?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — it feels like something is tickling or crawling, and I think I've seen one or two tiny things move when I part her hair.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Tiny greyish-white specks firmly adherent to the hair shafts near the scalp, clustered behind the ears and at the occiput, that do not flick or brush off
trigger_question_examples: Are there any white specks on the hairs, and do they brush off easily like dandruff or stay stuck?
Where on her head are the specks — near the scalp or further down the hair?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: There are little greyish-white specks stuck to the hairs close to the scalp, mostly behind her ears and at the back. They don't brush off like dandruff — they're really stuck on.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Excoriations from scratching across the scalp and posterior neck with some broken skin, but no thick yellow crusting, pus, or spreading redness reported
trigger_question_examples: Has the scratching broken the skin anywhere?
Is any of the skin weepy, crusty, or oozing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She's scratched the back of her scalp and neck raw, but I don't see any yellow crusts or pus and it's not spreading or hot.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, no generalised rash, and no systemic illness — the child is alert and feeding but exhausted and irritable
trigger_question_examples: Is she otherwise well in herself — eating, drinking, no rash elsewhere?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever that I've noticed and no rash anywhere else. She's eating and drinking fine, just tired and cranky from not sleeping.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Shared bedroom and bedding with younger siblings; caregiver worried about transmission to other household and school children
trigger_question_examples: Who else lives at home with her?
Has anyone else at home or school had an itchy head?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She shares a room and bedding with her younger brothers and sisters, and I'm worried they'll all get it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Caregiver already tried an over-the-counter anti-dandruff shampoo with no relief
trigger_question_examples: Have you tried anything for it already?
Did the shampoo help at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I tried an anti-dandruff shampoo from the chemist but it did absolutely nothing.
